rulings.Calendar Matter: RE-SET HEARING ON DEFENDANT THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2024, line 9, DEFENDANT THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT Respondent the Judicial Council of California's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Defense Firm
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2024, line 6, PLAINTIFF HILARY MCKEAN'S Motion To Disqualify Defense Firm Plaintiff's "motion to disqualify defense law firm" is denied. In this action, plaintiff alleges that her brother sexually assaulted her in the 1970s. Defendant subpoenaed documents from plaintiff's former employer (Ketchum, Inc.) and a spiritual healer (Deborah King Center, Inc.). The former subpoena appears noncontroversial. As to the latter, defense counsel acknowledges the parties agreed to a "first look" privilege review by plaintiff's counsel before the document production - a process that did not occur. Defense counsel is "thoroughly embarrassed" by his oversight and error. (Kipper Dec. 3:21:27.) Inadvertence is supported by the facts: e.g., plaintiff's counsel was alerted two days after the mistaken production and the 42 pages of King documents were deleted by defense counsel. (Id. at 3:21-5:7; Achermann Dec. 2:17-3:5; Memo. 3:20-4:5.) Factual scenarios in the cases plaintiff relies on are less benign. (See, e.g., State Comp. Ins. Fund v. WPS, Inc. (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 644; McDermott Will & Emery LLP v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 1083.) The court's denial of attorney disqualification does not preclude plaintiff from moving to exclude specific documents after the first look process is complete. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL B. POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 21, 2024, line 8, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LONDON BREED DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend. The FAC does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(e); see also People v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 1480, 1486 [Judicial Council form complaint no more immune from demurrer than any other that fails to meet pleading requirements].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)
